_meta.author: Peter Higgins / Claude
_meta.purpose: Machine-readable index of the EITT chemistry extension. Contains canonical claims, key numbers, file inventory, and provenance. For any AI starting EITT chemistry work: read this file first, then the markdown companions.

domain.name: EITT on the Chemical Simplex
domain.scope: Entropy Invariance under Temporal Transformation applied to chemical mixture compositions from the CheMixHub benchmark
domain.status: Complete — findings documented, PRISM allocation targets ranked, markdown companions available

canonical_claims._warning: These are the exact claims made by the EITT chemistry analysis. Numbers come from HUF_FAST_REFRESH.json proof_4_chemistry.

claim_1_partial_invariance.statement: Shannon entropy of chemical compositions is partially invariant under geometric-mean decimation. Interior pass rates 54-82%, overall ~53%.
claim_1_partial_invariance.comparison: Energy time series: 94.8%. The gap quantifies temporal autocorrelation's contribution.
claim_1_partial_invariance.qualification: Composition sweeps are not time series. Results describe EITT on smoothly ordered compositions.

claim_2_geometry_dynamics_split.statement: Approximately 50% of EITT invariance comes from Aitchison geometry of the simplex, approximately 50% from temporal autocorrelation.
claim_2_geometry_dynamics_split.evidence: Interior-only pass rates (54-82%) vs temporal EITT (94.8%). First empirical decomposition of the two contributions.
claim_2_geometry_dynamics_split.qualification: The 50/50 split is approximate, not a formal decomposition.

claim_3_boundary_failure.statement: Shannon entropy fails at the simplex boundary where min(x_i) < 0.05. Curvature of H(x) diverges as -1/x_i near zero.
claim_3_boundary_failure.evidence: All four lenses show degraded performance at boundary. NIST boundary: Shannon 35.7% pass at 12.12% residual.
claim_3_boundary_failure.consequence: Shannon-based QA metrics are geometrically unreliable for trace-level components.

claim_4_aitchison_boundary_rescue.statement: The Aitchison norm recovers performance at the boundary where Shannon fails. NIST boundary: 42.9% pass at 2.49% residual (vs Shannon 35.7% at 12.12%).
claim_4_aitchison_boundary_rescue.evidence: Table 1 in EITT_Chemistry_Findings.md. Polymer boundary: Aitchison 57.7% vs Shannon 42.3%.
claim_4_aitchison_boundary_rescue.implication: Boundary failures are metric artefacts, not failures of the underlying compositional structure.

claim_5_jensen_overcorrection.statement: The Jensen (Hessian-based) bias correction diverges catastrophically at the simplex boundary. 476% overcorrection at ionic-liquid boundary.
claim_5_jensen_overcorrection.evidence: Table 1, Jensen column. 0% pass rate at all boundary compositions.
claim_5_jensen_overcorrection.mechanism: The 1/x_i term in the Hessian dominates when x_i is small. Local approximation applied to global traversal.
claim_5_jensen_overcorrection.implication: Symmetric confidence intervals from Fisher information at the geometric mean are unreliable below 5%.

claim_6_dual_lens.statement: A natural dual-lens diagnostic emerges: Shannon for interior (optimal 82%), Aitchison for boundary (optimal 58%), crossover at min(x_i) ~ 0.05.
claim_6_dual_lens.evidence: Best-performing lens switches between Shannon (interior) and Aitchison (boundary) across all datasets.
claim_6_dual_lens.qualification: The 0.05 threshold is empirical. Formal derivation is an open problem (PRISM Target 7).

claim_7_prism_method.statement: PRISM converts EITT residuals into ranked resource allocation targets for analytical infrastructure investment.
claim_7_prism_method.evidence: PRISM_Chemistry_Analysis.md — 7 ranked targets from immediate (software update) to foundational (mathematical research).
claim_7_prism_method.novelty: First systematic entropy-invariance diagnostic applied to the global chemical industry ($5.7T annual revenue).

definitions._note: Explicit definitions for terms that cause drift or ambiguity.
definitions.entropy_formula: H(x) = -sum( x_i * ln(x_i) ) — natural logarithm, base e
definitions.geometric_mean_decimation: Group consecutive compositions into blocks of size k, compute the Aitchison barycenter (geometric mean with closure) of each block: x_bar_G = C( prod(x(t))^(1/k) )
definitions.pass_criterion: Relative residual |delta_H| / H < threshold (typically 5%). A group 'passes' EITT if entropy change under decimation is below this threshold.
definitions.interior: A composition where min(x_i) >= 0.05 — all parts above 5%
definitions.boundary: A composition where min(x_i) < 0.05 — at least one part below 5%
definitions.aitchison_distance: d_A(x,y) = ||clr(x) - clr(y)|| where clr(x) = ( ln(x_i) - (1/D) sum(ln(x_j)) )
definitions.simplex_closure: C(x) = x / sum(x_i) — project onto the unit simplex

provenance.eitt_origin: Peter Higgins / Rogue Wave Audio, confirmed on energy-market compositions (2025-2026)
provenance.aitchison_geometry: John Aitchison (1982), developed by Pawlowsky-Glahn, Egozcue, and CoDa community
provenance.chemixhub_data: Rajaonson et al., NeurIPS 2025 Datasets and Benchmarks Track
provenance.pipeline: chem_eitt_pipeline.py — open source, single-file Python, no proprietary dependencies
provenance.governance: HUF-GOV: Measure, report, file. No intervention on the data.
provenance.review: Multi-AI adversarial review (Claude, ChatGPT, Grok, Gemini, Copilot) applied throughout

document_family._note: The three primary documents form a coherent trilogy. Read in this order.

layer_1_empirical.title: EITT on the Chemical Simplex
layer_1_empirical.role: Raw science. What happened when we tested EITT on chemistry.

layer_2_interpretive.title: The HUF Development Index
layer_2_interpretive.role: What the residuals mean. Where chemistry sits on the development arc.

open_problems._note: From EITT_CoDa_Cheatsheet_v3.md Section 4.3 and PRISM targets.
open_problems.O-1: Formal proof of EITT invariance from Aitchison geometry
open_problems.O-2: Non-temporal EITT — test on spatial, cross-sectional, multi-modal compositions
open_problems.O-3: Higher-D simplex behaviour — how does EITT scale with dimension?
open_problems.O-4: Boundary threshold formalisation — derive the 0.05 crossover from curvature of H(x) on the D-simplex
open_problems.O-5: Jensen damping coefficient — proportional to composition range, keeps Hessian correction within validity

honest_disclosures: Composition sweeps are not time series. Results describe EITT on smoothly ordered compositions.
Binary mixtures (D=2) live on the one-dimensional simplex. Higher-D tests limited by data availability.
CheMixHub data is curated for ML benchmarking. Preprocessing may affect compositional structure.
The 0.05 boundary threshold is empirical, not theoretically derived.
EITT is an empirical observation, not a theorem. Formal proof is Open Problem O-1.
PRISM return-on-investment rankings are qualitative estimates, not formal economic analysis.
The author is the originator of HUF and EITT. This is first-party analysis, not independent audit.
